arabic: صٓۚ وَٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ ذِي ٱلذِّكۡرِ
transliteration: Sad waalqurani thee alththikri
translation: Sad . By this Qur’aan, that contains all that one should remember and all that matters!
notes: This is one of the letters of the Arabic language that appear at the beginning of some Qur’aanic chapters. Significance of these letters is a mystery. Omniscient Allah has perhaps purposely kept it mysterious to remind mankind of the limitation of knowledge it is bestowed with.

arabic: بَلِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ فِي عِزَّةٖ وَشِقَاقٖ
transliteration: Bali allatheena kafaroo fee AAizzatin washiqaqin
translation: But, verily, the suppressors of the Truth are lost in false pride, and deeply out of tune with Reality.

arabic: كَمۡ أَهۡلَكۡنَا مِن قَبۡلِهِم مِّن قَرۡنٖ فَنَادَواْ وَّلَاتَ حِينَ مَنَاصٖ
transliteration: Kam ahlakna min qablihim min qarnin fanadaw walata heena manasin
translation: How many a generation have We destroyed before them! They (the earlier generations) prayed to Us when it was too late for them to escape!

arabic: وَعَجِبُوٓاْ أَن جَآءَهُم مُّنذِرٞ مِّنۡهُمۡۖ وَقَالَ ٱلۡكَٰفِرُونَ هَٰذَا سَٰحِرٞ كَذَّابٌ
transliteration: WaAAajiboo an jaahum munthirun minhum waqala alkafiroona hatha sahirun kaththabun
translation: And people deem it strange that a person from among themselves should have come to warn them! And the suppressors of the Truth say, “This one is but a lying magician!”

arabic: أَجَعَلَ ٱلۡأٓلِهَةَ إِلَٰهٗا وَٰحِدًاۖ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَشَيۡءٌ عُجَابٞ
transliteration: AjaAAala alalihata ilahan wahidan inna hatha lashayon AAujabun
translation: “Has he converted all deities into one single God? This is indeed a strange thing!”

arabic: وَٱنطَلَقَ ٱلۡمَلَأُ مِنۡهُمۡ أَنِ ٱمۡشُواْ وَٱصۡبِرُواْ عَلَىٰٓ ءَالِهَتِكُمۡۖ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَشَيۡءٞ يُرَادُ
transliteration: Waintalaqa almalao minhum ani imshoo waisbiroo AAala alihatikum inna hatha lashayon yuradu
translation: And the leaders among them egged them on, “Go and persevere with your deities. This (what Muhammad is propounding) is indeed a thing designed!”

arabic: مَا سَمِعۡنَا بِهَٰذَا فِي ٱلۡمِلَّةِ ٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ إِنۡ هَٰذَآ إِلَّا ٱخۡتِلَٰقٌ
transliteration: Ma samiAAna bihatha fee almillati alakhirati in hatha illa ikhtilaqun
translation: “We have not heard of this in any recent society! This is nothing but fabrication!”

arabic: أَءُنزِلَ عَلَيۡهِ ٱلذِّكۡرُ مِنۢ بَيۡنِنَاۚ بَلۡ هُمۡ فِي شَكّٖ مِّن ذِكۡرِيۚ بَل لَّمَّا يَذُوقُواْ عَذَابِ
transliteration: Aonzila AAalayhi alththikru min baynina bal hum fee shakkin min thikree bal lamma yathooqoo AAathabi
translation: “Is it upon him – from amongst us all – that the divine Scripture has been sent down!?” Nay! It is My Scripture that they doubt. Nay! They have not tasted the punishment I give.

arabic: أَمۡ عِندَهُمۡ خَزَآئِنُ رَحۡمَةِ رَبِّكَ ٱلۡعَزِيزِ ٱلۡوَهَّابِ
transliteration: Am AAindahum khazainu rahmati rabbika alAAazeezi alwahhabi
translation: Or, do they have the treasures of your Lord’s Mercy – the Lord Omnipotent, the One Who bestows grace!?

arabic: أَمۡ لَهُم مُّلۡكُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَاۖ فَلۡيَرۡتَقُواْ فِي ٱلۡأَسۡبَٰبِ
transliteration: Am lahum mulku alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma falyartaqoo fee alasbabi
translation: Or, do they possess the absolute sovereignty over the heavens and the earth and all that is in between!? Let them then ascend in vehicles!

arabic: جُندٞ مَّا هُنَالِكَ مَهۡزُومٞ مِّنَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابِ
transliteration: Jundun ma hunalika mahzoomun mina alahzabi
translation: The confederate forces are bound to suffer defeat there.
notes: After the Muslims generally abandoned the Qur’aan to be their Guide, Allah Almighty, it appears, passed the investigative bent of human mind from them to others. This is apparent from the fact that while the leadership in scientific investigation lay with the Muslims for centuries after revelation of the Qur’aan, that leadership is no longer with them now. It is now in the hands of the atheists. These atheists think that they can conquer and rule the universe. They have succeeded, obviously with Allah’s hidden permission, to cross the earth’s atmosphere into outer space. The divine permission is clearly hinted at in Verse 10 above, although at the time of the revelation of the Qur’aan nobody had imagined that man would be able to land on the moon. Allah obviously permitted man to have forays into outer space just to enable him to have a glimpse of His immense superhuman power in building the universe. Allah obviously wanted man to know for certain that the task is far beyond human abilities. Yet, the human scientist remains adamantly atheistic! The Satan goads him to dream of conquering the universe. It’s just his wishful thinking as Verse 11 above informs us.

arabic: كَذَّبَتۡ قَبۡلَهُمۡ قَوۡمُ نُوحٖ وَعَادٞ وَفِرۡعَوۡنُ ذُو ٱلۡأَوۡتَادِ
transliteration: Kaththabat qablahum qawmu noohin waAAadun wafirAAawnu thoo alawtadi
translation: Before them, Noah’s people, and the tribe of AAad, and Pharaoh of the many tent-pegs (i.e. with means and power), denied the Truth,

arabic: وَثَمُودُ وَقَوۡمُ لُوطٖ وَأَصۡحَٰبُ لۡـَٔيۡكَةِۚ أُوْلَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابُ
transliteration: Wathamoodu waqawmu lootin waashabu alaykati olaika alahzabu
translation: And so did the tribe of Thamood, and the people of Lot, and the dwellers of the wood [Midian]: those were the peoples allied against the Truth.

arabic: إِن كُلٌّ إِلَّا كَذَّبَ ٱلرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ عِقَابِ
transliteration: In kullun illa kaththaba alrrusula fahaqqa AAiqabi
translation: Not one among them but denied the Messengers. So, they deserved My penalty.

arabic: وَمَا يَنظُرُ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ إِلَّا صَيۡحَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗ مَّا لَهَا مِن فَوَاقٖ
transliteration: Wama yanthuru haolai illa sayhatan wahidatan ma laha min fawaqin
translation: And these people (of this age) need wait for no more than one single blast (to be annihilated).
notes: The World War II atomic bomb blasts at Nagasaki and Hiroshima in Japan provide samples of such total annihilation.

arabic: وَقَالُواْ رَبَّنَا عَجِّل لَّنَا قِطَّنَا قَبۡلَ يَوۡمِ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
transliteration: Waqaloo rabbana AAajjil lana qittana qabla yawmi alhisabi
translation: And they (the polytheists) say (mockingly), “Our Lord! Hasten on to us our share before the Day of Reckoning!”

arabic: ٱصۡبِرۡ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَٱذۡكُرۡ عَبۡدَنَا دَاوُۥدَ ذَا ٱلۡأَيۡدِۖ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ
transliteration: Isbir AAala ma yaqooloona waothkur AAabdana dawooda tha alaydi innahu awwabun
translation: Bear patiently what they (the polytheists) say, and remember Our devotee David, a man of strength. He was indeed of those who always turned to Us.

arabic: إِنَّا سَخَّرۡنَا ٱلۡجِبَالَ مَعَهُۥ يُسَبِّحۡنَ بِٱلۡعَشِيِّ وَٱلۡإِشۡرَاقِ
transliteration: Inna sakhkharna aljibala maAAahu yusabbihna bialAAashiyyi waalishraqi
translation: We did indeed cause the mountains to join him (David) in extolling Our Glory at sunset and at sunrise,

arabic: وَٱلطَّيۡرَ مَحۡشُورَةٗۖ كُلّٞ لَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٞ
transliteration: Waalttayra mahshooratan kullun lahu awwabun
translation: And the birds in their assemblies: they all would always turn to Him.

arabic: وَشَدَدۡنَا مُلۡكَهُۥ وَءَاتَيۡنَٰهُ ٱلۡحِكۡمَةَ وَفَصۡلَ ٱلۡخِطَابِ
transliteration: Washadadna mulkahu waataynahu alhikmata wafasla alkhitabi
translation: And We strengthened his (David’s) kingdom and bestowed upon him wisdom and decisiveness in speech.

arabic: ۞وَهَلۡ أَتَىٰكَ نَبَؤُاْ ٱلۡخَصۡمِ إِذۡ تَسَوَّرُواْ ٱلۡمِحۡرَابَ
transliteration: Wahal ataka nabao alkhasmi ith tasawwaroo almihraba
translation: And has the information of the litigants come to your knowledge when they climbed over the walls of (King David’s) private chamber?

arabic: إِذۡ دَخَلُواْ عَلَىٰ دَاوُۥدَ فَفَزِعَ مِنۡهُمۡۖ قَالُواْ لَا تَخَفۡۖ خَصۡمَانِ بَغَىٰ بَعۡضُنَا عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٖ فَٱحۡكُم بَيۡنَنَا بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا تُشۡطِطۡ وَٱهۡدِنَآ إِلَىٰ سَوَآءِ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ
transliteration: Ith dakhaloo AAala dawooda fafaziAAa minhum qaloo la takhaf khasmani bagha baAAduna AAala baAAdin faohkum baynana bialhaqqi wala tushtit waihdina ila sawai alssirati
translation: When they confronted David, he shrank back in fear of them. They said, “Fear not! We are two litigants. One of us has wronged the other. So, judge between us with justice, and deviate not from what is right, and show both of us the right path.”

arabic: إِنَّ هَٰذَآ أَخِي لَهُۥ تِسۡعٞ وَتِسۡعُونَ نَعۡجَةٗ وَلِيَ نَعۡجَةٞ وَٰحِدَةٞ فَقَالَ أَكۡفِلۡنِيهَا وَعَزَّنِي فِي ٱلۡخِطَابِ
transliteration: Inna hatha akhee lahu tisAAun watisAAoona naAAjatan waliya naAAjatun wahidatun faqala akfilneeha waAAazzanee fee alkhitabi
translation: “This man here is my brother. He has ninety-nine ewes, whereas I have one ewe. And he asks me to hand over to him even the one I have. And he prevailed over me in the discourse we had.”

arabic: قَالَ لَقَدۡ ظَلَمَكَ بِسُؤَالِ نَعۡجَتِكَ إِلَىٰ نِعَاجِهِۦۖ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرٗا مِّنَ ٱلۡخُلَطَآءِ لَيَبۡغِي بَعۡضُهُمۡ عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٍ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ وَقَلِيلٞ مَّا هُمۡۗ وَظَنَّ دَاوُۥدُ أَنَّمَا فَتَنَّـٰهُ فَٱسۡتَغۡفَرَ رَبَّهُۥ وَخَرَّۤ رَاكِعٗاۤ وَأَنَابَ۩
transliteration: Qala laqad thalamaka bisuali naAAjatika ila niAAajihi wainna katheeran mina alkhulatai layabghee baAAduhum AAala baAAdin illa allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati waqaleelun ma hum wathanna dawoodu annama fatannahu faistaghfara rabbahu wakharra rakiAAan waanaba
translation: David said (to the complainant), “He has certainly wronged you by demanding that your ewe be added to his ewes! And indeed do many associates – except those who believe in Allah and do righteous deeds – wrong one another; but how few are those exceptions!” And David at once realized that We had tested him (by presenting to him the case of the ewes)! And so he asked his Lord to forgive him, and bowed down, and turned to Him in repentance.
notes: In all probability the two litigants were angels sent by Allah Ta’ala to teach King David a lesson. Undoubtedly, the King had committed some unnamed impropriety, unbecoming of a Prophet King of his high stature. A safe bet is to assume that the King’s impropriety was of the nature of the impropriety involved in the litigants’ case. But since Allah almighty has thought it fit not to specifically name the King’s impropriety, we should better avoid echoing what Chritian scriptures (Old Testament) unabashedly accuse the King of committing an undoubtedly sinful act. The accusation must be patently false since Allah Almighty could not have chosen a man committing such a heinous crime as His Prophet. The Christian scripture containing this accusation is not purely divine as the Qur’aan is. It is of the nature of the man-influenced and error-prone ahaadeeth which too contain some unpalatable and unsavoury things about our own Prophet Muhammad (peace on him). What King David might have done could only be described as an impropriety not amounting to sin. And as the next Verse below informs us Allah forgave King David.

arabic: فَغَفَرۡنَا لَهُۥ ذَٰلِكَۖ وَإِنَّ لَهُۥ عِندَنَا لَزُلۡفَىٰ وَحُسۡنَ مَـَٔابٖ
transliteration: Faghafarna lahu thalika wainna lahu AAindana lazulfa wahusna maabin
translation: And We forgave him (David) that [the impropriety that he had committed]. And, indeed, he will certainly be among those near to us – the best place to return to!

arabic: يَٰدَاوُۥدُ إِنَّا جَعَلۡنَٰكَ خَلِيفَةٗ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ فَٱحۡكُم بَيۡنَ ٱلنَّاسِ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا تَتَّبِعِ ٱلۡهَوَىٰ فَيُضِلَّكَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَضِلُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ لَهُمۡ عَذَابٞ شَدِيدُۢ بِمَا نَسُواْ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
transliteration: Ya dawoodu inna jaAAalnaka khaleefatan fee alardi faohkum bayna alnnasi bialhaqqi wala tattabiAAi alhawa fayudillaka AAan sabeeli Allahi inna allatheena yadilloona AAan sabeeli Allahi lahum AAathabun shadeedun bima nasoo yawma alhisabi
translation: “O David! We have indeed made you a vicegerent on earth. Establish, then, a just rule among the people, and follow not vain desire, lest it lead you astray from Allah’s Path. Those who go astray from Allah’s Path shall indeed suffer severe punishment for having forgotten the Day of Reckoning!”

arabic: وَمَا خَلَقۡنَا ٱلسَّمَآءَ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا بَٰطِلٗاۚ ذَٰلِكَ ظَنُّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْۚ فَوَيۡلٞ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِنَ ٱلنَّارِ
transliteration: Wama khalaqna alssamaa waalarda wama baynahuma batilan thalika thannu allatheena kafaroo fawaylun lillatheena kafaroo mina alnnari
translation: AND We have not created the heaven and the earth and all that is in between in vain. That is what those who suppress the Truth think. Woe then from the Fire unto all those who suppress the Truth!

arabic: أَمۡ نَجۡعَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ كَٱلۡمُفۡسِدِينَ فِي ٱلۡأَرۡضِ أَمۡ نَجۡعَلُ ٱلۡمُتَّقِينَ كَٱلۡفُجَّارِ
transliteration: Am najAAalu allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati kaalmufsideena fee alardi am najAAalu almuttaqeena kaalfujjari
translation: Would We treat those who believe and do righteous deeds like those who spread corruption on the earth!? Would We treat those who are Allah-fearing like those that are wicked!?

arabic: كِتَٰبٌ أَنزَلۡنَٰهُ إِلَيۡكَ مُبَٰرَكٞ لِّيَدَّبَّرُوٓاْ ءَايَٰتِهِۦ وَلِيَتَذَكَّرَ أُوْلُواْ ٱلۡأَلۡبَٰبِ
transliteration: Kitabun anzalnahu ilayka mubarakun liyaddabbaroo ayatihi waliyatathakkara oloo alalbabi
translation: Blessed is the Book We have revealed to you, [O Muhammad,] so that people may ponder over its Verses, and that those who are endowed with insight may pass on their understanding to others.

arabic: وَوَهَبۡنَا لِدَاوُۥدَ سُلَيۡمَٰنَۚ نِعۡمَ ٱلۡعَبۡدُ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ
transliteration: Wawahabna lidawooda sulaymana niAAma alAAabdu innahu awwabun
translation: And We bestowed Solomon – an excellent devotee – [as a son] to David. He would indeed always turn to Us.

arabic: إِذۡ عُرِضَ عَلَيۡهِ بِٱلۡعَشِيِّ ٱلصَّـٰفِنَٰتُ ٱلۡجِيَادُ
transliteration: Ith AAurida AAalayhi bialAAashiyyi alssafinatu aljiyadu
translation: As, towards the close of day, well-bred horses were brought before him (Solomon),

arabic: فَقَالَ إِنِّيٓ أَحۡبَبۡتُ حُبَّ ٱلۡخَيۡرِ عَن ذِكۡرِ رَبِّي حَتَّىٰ تَوَارَتۡ بِٱلۡحِجَابِ
transliteration: Faqala innee ahbabtu hubba alkhayri AAan thikri rabbee hatta tawarat bialhijabi
translation: He (Solomon) said, “I have indeed come to love the good things in life as these remind me of my Lord (Who has created them)!” And when they (the horses) were out of sight under the veil of darkness (of the night),

arabic: رُدُّوهَا عَلَيَّۖ فَطَفِقَ مَسۡحَۢا بِٱلسُّوقِ وَٱلۡأَعۡنَاقِ
transliteration: Ruddooha AAalayya fatafiqa mashan bialssooqi waalaAAnaqi
translation: (Solomon ordered,) “Bring them (the horses) back to me!” And then he would [lovingly] stroke their legs and their necks.

arabic: وَلَقَدۡ فَتَنَّا سُلَيۡمَٰنَ وَأَلۡقَيۡنَا عَلَىٰ كُرۡسِيِّهِۦ جَسَدٗا ثُمَّ أَنَابَ
transliteration: Walaqad fatanna sulaymana waalqayna AAala kursiyyihi jasadan thumma anaba
translation: And We did certainly put Solomon on trial by placing upon his throne a [lifeless] body; and thereupon he turned in repentance to Us.
notes: Like King David, King Solomon too, it seems, had committed some unnamed impropriety. Books of Ahaadeeth do allude to some highly impossible and improbable acts attributed to the great King. But the Ahaadeeth are man-influenced and error- prone. And why should we go to those books when the Qur’aan gives us everything needed for our guidance? Allah Almighty has thought it fit not to specifically mention the impropriety done, so why should mankind be eager to find it out? This Qur’aanic Verse itself indicates that the impropriety was related to the succession to Solomon’s throne. We learn from Israelite history that the successor to Solomon was incompetent and that lead to the disintegration of the great Solomon Empire. What we should learn from these episodes concerning the two Prophet-kings is that (i) Prophets are after all human and (ii) hereditary succession to ruler-ship is not divinely sanctioned.

arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱغۡفِرۡ لِي وَهَبۡ لِي مُلۡكٗا لَّا يَنۢبَغِي لِأَحَدٖ مِّنۢ بَعۡدِيٓۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلۡوَهَّابُ
transliteration: Qala rabbi ighfir lee wahab lee mulkan la yanbaghee liahadin min baAAdee innaka anta alwahhabu
translation: He (Solomon) prayed, “O my Lord! Forgive me my sins, and bestow upon me a kingdom which may not suit anyone after me. You are indeed the One Who bestows gifts!”

arabic: فَسَخَّرۡنَا لَهُ ٱلرِّيحَ تَجۡرِي بِأَمۡرِهِۦ رُخَآءً حَيۡثُ أَصَابَ
transliteration: Fasakhkharna lahu alrreeha tajree biamrihi rukhaan haythu asaba
translation: We then made the wind subservient to him, so that it blew gently at his behest in the direction he willed.

arabic: وَٱلشَّيَٰطِينَ كُلَّ بَنَّآءٖ وَغَوَّاصٖ
transliteration: Waalshshayateena kulla bannain waghawwasin
translation: And (We made) the devils (work for him) in every kind of construction activity and in diving.

arabic: وَءَاخَرِينَ مُقَرَّنِينَ فِي ٱلۡأَصۡفَادِ
transliteration: Waakhareena muqarraneena fee alasfadi
translation: And there were others too (who worked for him while) linked together in fetters.

arabic: هَٰذَا عَطَآؤُنَا فَٱمۡنُنۡ أَوۡ أَمۡسِكۡ بِغَيۡرِ حِسَابٖ
transliteration: Hatha AAataona faomnun aw amsik bighayri hisabin
translation: [And We told him,] “This is Our special Grant; whether you help others with it or not, no account will be taken!”

arabic: وَإِنَّ لَهُۥ عِندَنَا لَزُلۡفَىٰ وَحُسۡنَ مَـَٔابٖ
transliteration: Wainna lahu AAindana lazulfa wahusna maabin
translation: And, indeed, he (Solomon) will certainly be among those near to us – the best place to return to!

arabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ عَبۡدَنَآ أَيُّوبَ إِذۡ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّي مَسَّنِيَ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنُ بِنُصۡبٖ وَعَذَابٍ
transliteration: Waothkur AAabdana ayyooba ith nada rabbahu annee massaniya alshshaytanu binusbin waAAathabin
translation: And recall when Our devotee Job prayed to his Lord, “The Satan has indeed afflicted me with illness and suffering!”
notes: Refer [21:83] & 21:84 and study note 22 (Qur’aanic Studies Manzil IV).

arabic: ٱرۡكُضۡ بِرِجۡلِكَۖ هَٰذَا مُغۡتَسَلُۢ بَارِدٞ وَشَرَابٞ
transliteration: Orkud birijlika hatha mughtasalun baridun washarabun
translation: [He was told,] “Stamp your foot!” [Water springs out from the ground.] “This is cool water to wash with and to drink!”

arabic: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥٓ أَهۡلَهُۥ وَمِثۡلَهُم مَّعَهُمۡ رَحۡمَةٗ مِّنَّا وَذِكۡرَىٰ لِأُوْلِي ٱلۡأَلۡبَٰبِ
transliteration: Wawahabna lahu ahlahu wamithlahum maAAahum rahmatan minna wathikra liolee alalbabi
translation: And We restored to him his family and as many more with them, as an act of grace from Us, and as a source of inspiration to all who are endowed with insight.

arabic: وَخُذۡ بِيَدِكَ ضِغۡثٗا فَٱضۡرِب بِّهِۦ وَلَا تَحۡنَثۡۗ إِنَّا وَجَدۡنَٰهُ صَابِرٗاۚ نِّعۡمَ ٱلۡعَبۡدُ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٞ
transliteration: Wakhuth biyadika dighthan faidrib bihi wala tahnath inna wajadnahu sabiran niAAma alAAabdu innahu awwabun
translation: [And We told him,] “Now take a handful of herbs and strike therewith, and you shall not break your oath!” We did indeed find him patient in adversity – an excellent devotee, who would always turn to Us!
notes: Apparently, during his illness, Job (Ayub) had taken an oath to give lashes to somebody he was then annoyed with. But after his recovery and restoration of his former happy position in life, Job was reluctant to carry out the oath, but he also did not want to be guilty of breaking an oath. And this was the via media divinely suggested to him. The lashes with the light bunch of herbs would not hurt the victim.

arabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ عِبَٰدَنَآ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسۡحَٰقَ وَيَعۡقُوبَ أُوْلِي ٱلۡأَيۡدِي وَٱلۡأَبۡصَٰرِ
transliteration: Waothkur AAibadana ibraheema waishaqa wayaAAqooba olee alaydee waalabsari
translation: And recall Our servants Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, who were all endowed with inner strength and vision:

arabic: إِنَّآ أَخۡلَصۡنَٰهُم بِخَالِصَةٖ ذِكۡرَى ٱلدَّارِ
transliteration: Inna akhlasnahum bikhalisatin thikra alddari
translation: We did indeed purify them by means of the purity of the remembrance of the life to come.

arabic: وَإِنَّهُمۡ عِندَنَا لَمِنَ ٱلۡمُصۡطَفَيۡنَ ٱلۡأَخۡيَارِ
transliteration: Wainnahum AAindana lamina almustafayna alakhyari
translation: And they were certainly indeed, in Our sight, among the selected best of humankind!

arabic: وَٱذۡكُرۡ إِسۡمَٰعِيلَ وَٱلۡيَسَعَ وَذَا ٱلۡكِفۡلِۖ وَكُلّٞ مِّنَ ٱلۡأَخۡيَارِ
transliteration: Waothkur ismaAAeela wailyasaAAa watha alkifli wakullun mina alakhyari
translation: And recall Ishmael and Elisha , and Zulkifl : every one of them was among the best of humankind!
notes:
Abraham’s elder son whom Abraham was ready to sacrifice at Allah’s command [see [37:101-107]].

Elisha [ ilyasaAAa ] is mentioned at one other place [[6:86]] in the Qur’aan, which gives no other details about him.

The Qur’aan gives no other details about this name too, except for a similar mention in [21:85].

arabic: هَٰذَا ذِكۡرٞۚ وَإِنَّ لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ لَحُسۡنَ مَـَٔابٖ
transliteration: Hatha thikrun wainna lilmuttaqeena lahusna maabin
translation: This [Qur’aan] is a Reminder/Narrative [of all that leads mankind to Paradise and all that leads them to Hell]. And, certainly indeed, the most beauteous of all goals awaits those who are pious and Allah-fearing.

arabic: جَنَّـٰتِ عَدۡنٖ مُّفَتَّحَةٗ لَّهُمُ ٱلۡأَبۡوَٰبُ
transliteration: Jannati AAadnin mufattahatan lahumu alabwabu
translation: The goals are gardens of perpetual bliss with gates kept open for them [the pious and Allah-fearing].

arabic: مُتَّكِـِٔينَ فِيهَا يَدۡعُونَ فِيهَا بِفَٰكِهَةٖ كَثِيرَةٖ وَشَرَابٖ
transliteration: Muttakieena feeha yadAAoona feeha bifakihatin katheeratin washarabin
translation: They will be comfortable and at ease therein, frequently calling for fruits and drinks.

arabic: ۞وَعِندَهُمۡ قَٰصِرَٰتُ ٱلطَّرۡفِ أَتۡرَابٌ
transliteration: WaAAindahum qasiratu alttarfi atrabun
translation: And they will have with them companions of modest gaze.

arabic: هَٰذَا مَا تُوعَدُونَ لِيَوۡمِ ٱلۡحِسَابِ
transliteration: Hatha ma tooAAadoona liyawmi alhisabi
translation: This is what you are promised for the Day of Reckoning:

arabic: إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَرِزۡقُنَا مَا لَهُۥ مِن نَّفَادٍ
transliteration: Inna hatha larizquna ma lahu min nafadin
translation: This, certainly indeed, shall be Our inexhaustible provision!

arabic: هَٰذَاۚ وَإِنَّ لِلطَّـٰغِينَ لَشَرَّ مَـَٔابٖ
transliteration: Hatha wainna lilttagheena lasharra maabin
translation: This [provision would of course be there as promised!] And, certainly indeed, the evilest of all goals awaits those who transgress the bounds of what is right.

arabic: جَهَنَّمَ يَصۡلَوۡنَهَا فَبِئۡسَ ٱلۡمِهَادُ
transliteration: Jahannama yaslawnaha fabisa almihadu
translation: Hell! They will suffer therein. And how vile a resting-place!

arabic: هَٰذَا فَلۡيَذُوقُوهُ حَمِيمٞ وَغَسَّاقٞ
transliteration: Hatha falyathooqoohu hameemun waghassaqun
translation: This, [then, is for them.] So, let them taste it: boiling water (to drink) and a pus-like thing (to eat!)

arabic: وَءَاخَرُ مِن شَكۡلِهِۦٓ أَزۡوَٰجٌ
transliteration: Waakharu min shaklihi azwajun
translation: And other torments in pairs, of similar appearance.

arabic: هَٰذَا فَوۡجٞ مُّقۡتَحِمٞ مَّعَكُمۡ لَا مَرۡحَبَۢا بِهِمۡۚ إِنَّهُمۡ صَالُواْ ٱلنَّارِ
transliteration: Hatha fawjun muqtahimun maAAakum la marhaban bihim innahum saloo alnnari
translation: This crowd of people who went headlong with you into sin – no welcome to them either! They shall indeed suffer the Fire!

arabic: قَالُواْ بَلۡ أَنتُمۡ لَا مَرۡحَبَۢا بِكُمۡۖ أَنتُمۡ قَدَّمۡتُمُوهُ لَنَاۖ فَبِئۡسَ ٱلۡقَرَارُ
transliteration: Qaloo bal antum la marhaban bikum antum qaddamtumoohu lana fabisa alqararu
translation: They [the seduced] will say [to the seducers], “Nay, but it is you who are not welcome! You have brought this upon us: and it is a vile state to be in!”

arabic: قَالُواْ رَبَّنَا مَن قَدَّمَ لَنَا هَٰذَا فَزِدۡهُ عَذَابٗا ضِعۡفٗا فِي ٱلنَّارِ
transliteration: Qaloo rabbana man qaddama lana hatha fazidhu AAathaban diAAfan fee alnnari
translation: They will say, “Our Lord! Increase the punishment in the Fire twofold for those who brought this upon us!”

arabic: وَقَالُواْ مَا لَنَا لَا نَرَىٰ رِجَالٗا كُنَّا نَعُدُّهُم مِّنَ ٱلۡأَشۡرَارِ
transliteration: Waqaloo ma lana la nara rijalan kunna naAAudduhum mina alashrari
translation: And they will say, “How come we do not see men whom we counted among the evil?”

arabic: أَتَّخَذۡنَٰهُمۡ سِخۡرِيًّا أَمۡ زَاغَتۡ عَنۡهُمُ ٱلۡأَبۡصَٰرُ
transliteration: Attakhathnahum sikhriyyan am zaghat AAanhumu alabsaru
translation: “We used to hold them in ridicule! Or is it that our eyes have missed them?”

arabic: إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ لَحَقّٞ تَخَاصُمُ أَهۡلِ ٱلنَّارِ
transliteration: Inna thalika lahaqqun takhasumu ahli alnnari
translation: That, indeed, will in truth be the mutual wrangling among the people of the Fire!

arabic: قُلۡ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ مُنذِرٞۖ وَمَا مِنۡ إِلَٰهٍ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ٱلۡوَٰحِدُ ٱلۡقَهَّارُ
transliteration: Qul innama ana munthirun wama min ilahin illa Allahu alwahidu alqahharu
translation: Say [O Muhammad], “I am only a warner! And there is none worthy of worship other than Allah, the One and Only, the Almighty.”

arabic: رَبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا ٱلۡعَزِيزُ ٱلۡغَفَّـٰرُ
transliteration: Rabbu alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma alAAazeezu alghaffaru
translation: “The Lord of the heavens and the earth and all that is in between, the Omnipotent, the Ultimate Forgiver!”

arabic: قُلۡ هُوَ نَبَؤٌاْ عَظِيمٌ
transliteration: Qul huwa nabaon AAatheemun
translation: Say: “This is a Message of the greatest importance!”

arabic: أَنتُمۡ عَنۡهُ مُعۡرِضُونَ
transliteration: Antum AAanhu muAAridoona
translation: “[And] you turn away from it!”

arabic: مَا كَانَ لِيَ مِنۡ عِلۡمِۭ بِٱلۡمَلَإِ ٱلۡأَعۡلَىٰٓ إِذۡ يَخۡتَصِمُونَ
transliteration: Ma kana liya min AAilmin bialmalai alaAAla ith yakhtasimoona
translation: “I could have had no knowledge of the angels up there when they argued [against the creation of man].”

arabic: إِن يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَيَّ إِلَّآ أَنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ نَذِيرٞ مُّبِينٌ
transliteration: In yooha ilayya illa annama ana natheerun mubeenun
translation: “[I could have no knowledge of it] if it had not been revealed to me [by Allah’s permission.] I am but a plain warner.”

arabic: إِذۡ قَالَ رَبُّكَ لِلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ إِنِّي خَٰلِقُۢ بَشَرٗا مِّن طِينٖ
transliteration: Ith qala rabbuka lilmalaikati innee khaliqun basharan min teenin
translation: When your Lord said to the angels, “I am indeed about to create a human being out of clay.”

arabic: فَإِذَا سَوَّيۡتُهُۥ وَنَفَخۡتُ فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِي فَقَعُواْ لَهُۥ سَٰجِدِينَ
transliteration: Faitha sawwaytuhu wanafakhtu feehi min roohee faqaAAoo lahu sajideena
translation: “And when I have completed his form and breathed into him of My spirit, lie down before him in prostration!”

arabic: فَسَجَدَ ٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ كُلُّهُمۡ أَجۡمَعُونَ
transliteration: Fasajada almalaikatu kulluhum ajmaAAoona
translation: The angels then prostrated all of them together.

arabic: إِلَّآ إِبۡلِيسَ ٱسۡتَكۡبَرَ وَكَانَ مِنَ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ
transliteration: Illa ibleesa istakbara wakana mina alkafireena
translation: [All] except Iblees , who became arrogant, and [thus] became one of those who suppress the Truth.
notes: Refer study notes 2.27 to 2.30 on Verse 2.34 (Manzil I).

arabic: قَالَ يَـٰٓإِبۡلِيسُ مَا مَنَعَكَ أَن تَسۡجُدَ لِمَا خَلَقۡتُ بِيَدَيَّۖ أَسۡتَكۡبَرۡتَ أَمۡ كُنتَ مِنَ ٱلۡعَالِينَ
transliteration: Qala ya ibleesu ma manaAAaka an tasjuda lima khalaqtu biyadayya astakbarta am kunta mina alAAaleena
translation: Allah said, “O Iblees! What has prevented you from prostrating before what I have created with My hands? Are you proud or are you of those who think of themselves as high and mighty!?”

arabic: قَالَ أَنَا۠ خَيۡرٞ مِّنۡهُ خَلَقۡتَنِي مِن نَّارٖ وَخَلَقۡتَهُۥ مِن طِينٖ
transliteration: Qala ana khayrun minhu khalaqtanee min narin wakhalaqtahu min teenin
translation: Iblees replied, “I am better than he. You have created me out of fire [energy], and You have created him out of clay [matter].”

arabic: قَالَ فَٱخۡرُجۡ مِنۡهَا فَإِنَّكَ رَجِيمٞ
transliteration: Qala faokhruj minha fainnaka rajeemun
translation: Allah said, “Get out from here; for, indeed, you are expelled!”

arabic: وَإِنَّ عَلَيۡكَ لَعۡنَتِيٓ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ ٱلدِّينِ
transliteration: Wainna AAalayka laAAnatee ila yawmi alddeeni
translation: “And My curse shall be on you till the Judgment Day!”

arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ فَأَنظِرۡنِيٓ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ يُبۡعَثُونَ
transliteration: Qala rabbi faanthirnee ila yawmi yubAAathoona
translation: Iblees said, “My Lord! Give me respite then till the Day of Resurrection!”

arabic: قَالَ فَإِنَّكَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُنظَرِينَ
transliteration: Qala fainnaka mina almunthareena
translation: Allah Ta’ala said, “So shall you indeed be among those given respite”

arabic: إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ ٱلۡوَقۡتِ ٱلۡمَعۡلُومِ
transliteration: Ila yawmi alwaqti almaAAloomi
translation: “For a period the duration of which is fixed.”

arabic: قَالَ فَبِعِزَّتِكَ لَأُغۡوِيَنَّهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
transliteration: Qala fabiAAizzatika laoghwiyannahum ajmaAAeena
translation: Iblees said, “Then I swear by Your Honour that I shall certainly seduce them all,”

arabic: إِلَّا عِبَادَكَ مِنۡهُمُ ٱلۡمُخۡلَصِينَ
transliteration: Illa AAibadaka minhumu almukhlaseena
translation: “All except those subjects of Yours who are sincerely devoted to You!”

arabic: قَالَ فَٱلۡحَقُّ وَٱلۡحَقَّ أَقُولُ
transliteration: Qala faalhaqqu waalhaqqa aqoolu
translation: Allah Ta’ala said, “The Truth, then – and in Truth do I state:”

arabic: لَأَمۡلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنكَ وَمِمَّن تَبِعَكَ مِنۡهُمۡ أَجۡمَعِينَ
transliteration: Laamlaanna jahannama minka wamimman tabiAAaka minhum ajmaAAeena
translation: “Certainly will I fill Hell with you and with all those who shall follow you among them!”

arabic: قُلۡ مَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٖ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلۡمُتَكَلِّفِينَ
transliteration: Qul ma asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin wama ana mina almutakallifeena
translation: Say [O Prophet], “I ask of you no reward for this [Qur’aan]. And I am not pretentious.”

arabic: إِنۡ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكۡرٞ لِّلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
transliteration: In huwa illa thikrun lilAAalameena
translation: “This [Qur’aan] is nothing but that which all human beings need to constantly refer to for rightful decisions and actions in their respective worlds.”

arabic: وَلَتَعۡلَمُنَّ نَبَأَهُۥ بَعۡدَ حِينِۭ
transliteration: WalataAAlamunna nabaahu baAAda heenin
translation: “And you will certainly come to know its [Qur’aan’s] Message [of the greatest importance] after some time! ”
notes:
See Verse 67 above.

Within a decade of the complete revelation of the Qur’aan, the Islamic State under Khalif Omar had already become the greatest power on earth. And during the next few centuries, Muslims had become the torch-bearers in almost all fields of human activity like science, medicine, technology etc. Muslims could have this achievement because they had then, by and large, grasped the importance of the Qur’aan and followed its tenets in their lives. The Muslims’ suzerainty however vanished after the majority among them started treating the Qur’aan as a thing of no importance! History has thus registered the importance of the Qur’aan in no uncertain terms. But man, alas, continues to be in deep slumber!
